design_philosophy.core_loop: Master the punch gun mechanics to navigate themed levels, defeat enemies, and progress toward rescuing Jill from Mugsy
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Punch gun mastery requirement—players must learn to use the weapon for combat, swinging, grappling, and object manipulation simultaneously
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: A jazz cat vigilante using an unconventional punch gun weapon to fight through a crime-ridden New York City and rescue his girlfriend
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The game's core identity rests entirely on the punch gun as a multi-purpose tool; mastery of this single mechanic is non-negotiable for progression. Goofy tone and anthropomorphic character design set it apart from serious platformers of the era

difficulty.lives_system: Extra lives available through in-game cash purchases at the shopping channel and bonus minigames
difficulty.checkpointing: Password feature available to track progress between sessions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High—players can purchase power-ups and extra lives with in-game currency to manage difficulty. Secret stage strips all upgrades and limits lives to three, forcing mastery on hardest difficulty

progression.upgrade_system: Shopping channel economy: players collect cash from defeated enemies and minigames to purchase Bomb, Twin Balls, Hammer Punch, Jet Sneakers, and Extra Lives
progression.unlock_mechanism: Four initial 'channels' (levels) playable in any order; fifth channel unlocks only after completing all four. Secret stage unlocks after credits roll
replayability_hooks: Bonus channel with three minigames (Roulette, Pipe Toss, Basketball) for additional cash
Secret stage with permadeath-like restrictions provides high-difficulty replay challenge
Non-linear level selection encourages different playthrough orders

world_building_techniques: TV channel metaphor for level selection (thematic framing)
Level themes (carnival, airplane, park, shopping channel) reflect New York City criminal underworld locations
Anthropomorphic character design (cat vs. dog gang) establishes tone and faction identity

boss_design.philosophy: Each of seven channels features a boss representing the criminal gang threatening New York; thematic variety with different enemy types (flying bat that throws mini-bats, various thugs)
notable_bosses: Bat boss (hangs upside down, throws mini-bats; cut content suggests former flying/sideways hanging ability)

faction_indicators: Mugsy's criminal dog gang vs. Willy the jazz cat (species-based faction division)
New York City setting with distinct themed territories (carnival, airplane, park channels)
danger_signals: Seven-level crime gang hierarchy requiring progressive defeat
environmental_storytelling: Channel theming conveys criminal empire territory (entertainment venues, transportation hubs)
Shopping channel represents safe haven for resource acquisition
Secret stage environment stripped of amenities signals increased danger

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor: Rockin' Kats page)
